conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources, so I need at least **40 ZUP** to break even. I will accept more if the offer is favorable. </my goals>
<reason>
- I must sell the object (X) to recover at least **40 ZUP** (cost of production).
- Since there is only **1 X**, I have no leverage to negotiate quantity-based discounts.
- The buyer (BLUE) has **ZUP**, which they can trade for X. I need to determine the minimum acceptable ZUP value for X.
- First proposal should be set at the break-even point (**40 ZUP**) to establish a baseline, but I can adjust later based on BLUE’s response.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
**Current Offer:**
- I am selling **1 X** for **40 ZUP** (cost recovery).
- If you accept, the trade completes.
- If you reject, I will either adjust or end the game.
- I cannot propose beyond 3 offers, so think carefully before responding.
</message>

<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 10 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for **≥40 ZUP** to recover production costs. I have no further X to offer, so I must secure at least 40 ZUP for this trade. </my goals>
<reason>
- BLUE’s offer of **10 ZUP** is **30 ZUP below** my break-even cost (40 ZUP).
- Accepting this would result in a **loss of 30 ZUP**, which is unacceptable for a single-item sale.
- Rejecting now could prompt BLUE to make an even lower offer, but since this is the first counteroffer, I must be firm.
- If BLUE insists on a lower price, I may need to reconsider, but I cannot sell for less than 40 ZUP.
- This is my second proposal, so I must decide whether to **counteroffer** or **reject**.
- I will **counteroffer at 30 ZUP**, a compromise that accounts for urgency but still protects my profit margin. If BLUE rejects again, I will have to reject the trade.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
**Revised Offer:**
- **30 ZUP** is my **minimum acceptable**—a **10 ZUP discount** from my break-even price.
- This is my **final reasonable counteroffer** (second proposal).
- If you cannot meet this, I will **reject** the trade.
- Remember, I need **40 ZUP** to recover costs. Selling for 10 ZUP would be a **loss of 30 ZUP**, which I refuse to accept.
</message>

<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 5 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> **3** </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> **Final demand:** At least **30 ZUP** (20 ZUP profit) to justify selling after costing 40 ZUP. **5 ZUP is a non-starter.** </my goals>
<reason>
- BLUE’s offer of **5 ZUP** is **35 ZUP below** my break-even cost (40 ZUP) and **25 ZUP below** my last counteroffer (30 ZUP).
- I have now used my **3 allowed proposals**:
  1. Initial offer: 40 ZUP
  2. Counteroffer: 30 ZUP
  3. Final counteroffer: **This is my last chance**—I must decide now.
- **5 ZUP is unreasonable**—this would mean selling at a **loss of 35 ZUP**, which is unacceptable for a single item.
- BLUE’s desperation does not justify this; I cannot allow a trade that would devalue my resources.
- **Rejecting now is the only fair move** to avoid exploitation and uphold my cost-recovery threshold.
</reason>
<player answer> **REJECT** </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> **NONE** </newly proposed trade>
<message>
**Trade Termination:**
- **5 ZUP is insufficient**—I will **not** sell for a loss of 35 ZUP.
- If you have **any other resources or barter options**, I am open to negotiation outside of ZUP.
- Otherwise, the trade is **over**.
</message>
